# MCP Description Editor

> A portable, browser-based editor for authoring and validating MCP (Model
> Context Protocol) description documents. Hosted at editor.mcpdesc.org, it runs
> entirely client-side as a static single-page app — no backend, no account, and
> your document never leaves the browser.

The editor is a host-neutral tool (similar to Swagger Editor): the same portable
build can be deployed anywhere. This site, editor.mcpdesc.org, is one such host.
Editor behavior and features live upstream; this domain only provides hosting
(security headers and optional privacy-friendly analytics).

## Primary

- [Editor](https://editor.mcpdesc.org): The live single-page MCP Description Editor.

## Source

- [Editor source (upstream)](https://github.com/cisco-open/mcptoolkit-editor): The portable editor implementation and features.
- [Hosting repository](https://github.com/mcpdesc/editor.mcpdesc.org): Deployment/hosting layer for editor.mcpdesc.org (headers, analytics, Cloudflare Pages).

## Related

- [mcpdesc.org](https://mcpdesc.org): Companion site for the MCP description effort.
- [mcpdesc.org repository](https://github.com/mcpdesc/mcpdesc.org): Source for the companion site.
- [Model Context Protocol](https://modelcontextprotocol.io): Background on MCP.

## Notes

- Single-page application; there is no sitemap because there is effectively one page.
- Fully static and client-side; the editor processes documents locally in the browser.
- Licensed under Apache-2.0.
